briefdescription.para: This macro waits for AES engine to complete key loading.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the AES engine completes key loading. XST_FAILURE if a timeout has occurred.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to zeroize the memory.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the memory which need to be zeroized.
Length of the data.

simplesect.para:
Final call of this API returns the status of Comparison.
XSECURE_CSU_AES_ZEROIZATION_ERROR: If Zeroization is not Successful.
XST_SUCCESS: If Zeroization is Successful.

briefdescription.para: This is a helper function to enable/disable byte swapping feature of CSU DMA.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XCsuDma instance.
Channel Type - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL
1 : Enable Byte Swapping 0 : Disable Byte Swapping

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, const u8 *SrcAddr, const u8 *DestAddr, u32 Length, u8 EnLastFlag)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ReadNPassChunkToAes

briefdescription.para: This API reads bitstream from flash device to destination address.

detaileddescription.para: This API should be called while loading bitstream in chunks.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
is address of encrypted data source location
is address of location where data will get copied
Length of data to be copied in bytes
is to indicate end of data

simplesect.para: returns XST_SUCCESS if successful, or the relevant errorcode

memberdef.argsstring: (XCsuDma *InstancePtr, const u8 *SrcAddr, u32 Length, u8 EnLastFlag)
memberdef.name: XSecure_PassChunkToAes

briefdescription.para: This API triggers DMA at source address.

detaileddescription.para: This API should be called while loading bitstream in chunks.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsuDma instance
is address of encrypted data source location
Length of data to be copied in bytes
is to indicate end of data

simplesect.para: returns XST_SUCCESS if successful, or the relevant errorcode

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the instance pointer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
Pointer to the XCsuDma instance.
Key source for decryption, can be KUP/device key (XSECURE_CSU_AES_KEY_SRC_KUP for KUP key or XSECURE_CSU_AES_KEY_SRC_DEV for Device Key)
Pointer to the Initialization Vector for decryption
Pointer to Aes key in case KUP key is used. Pass Null if the device key is to be used.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful.

All the inputs are accepted in little endian format but the AES engine accepts the data in big endian format, The decryption and encryption functions in xsecure_aes handle the little endian to big endian conversion using few API's, Xil_Htonl (provided by Xilinx xil_io library) and XSecure_AesCsuDmaConfigureEndiannes for handling data endianness conversions. If higher performance is needed, users can strictly use data in big endian format and modify the xsecure_aes functions to remove the use of the Xil_Htonl and XSecure_AesCsuDmaConfigureEndiannes functions as required.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to initialize the AES engine for encryption.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
Pointer of a buffer in which encrypted data along with GCM TAG will be stored. Buffer size should be Size of data plus 16 bytes.
A 32 bit variable, which holds the size of the input data to be encrypted in bytes, whereas number of bytes provided should be multiples of 4.

simplesect.para: None
If all of the data to be encrypted is available, the XSecure_AesEncryptData function can be used instead.

listitem.para: Clear AES contents by resetting it.
Clear AES_KEY_CLEAR bits to avoid clearing of key
Helion AES block expects the key in big-endian.
Enable CSU DMA Src channel for byte swapping.
Enable CSU DMA Dst channel for byte swapping.
Push IV into the AES engine.

briefdescription.para: This function encrypts the clear-text data passed in and updates the GCM tag from any previous calls.

detaileddescription.para: The size from XSecure_AesEncryptInit is decremented from the size passed into this function to determine when the final CSU DMA transfer of data to the AES-GCM cryptographic core.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
Pointer to the data for which encryption should be performed.
A 32 bit variable, which holds the size of the input data in bytes, whereas the number of bytes provided should be multiples of 4.

simplesect.para: None
If all of the data to be encrypted is available, the XSecure_AesEncryptData function can be used instead.

listitem.para: Disable CSU DMA Dst channel for byte swapping.
Disable CSU DMA Src channel for byte swapping.

briefdescription.para: This function encrypts Len (length) number of bytes of the passed in Src (source) buffer and stores the encrypted data along with its associated 16 byte tag in the Dst (destination) buffer.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
A pointer to a buffer where encrypted data along with GCM tag will be stored. The Size of buffer provided should be Size of the data plus 16 bytes
A pointer to input data for encryption.
Size of input data in bytes, whereas the number of bytes provided should be multiples of 4.

simplesect.para: None

If data to be encrypted is not available in one buffer one can call XSecure_AesEncryptInit() and update the AES engine with data to be encrypted by calling XSecure_AesEncryptUpdate() API multiple times as required.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the AES engine for decryption and is required to be called before calling XSecure_AesDecryptUpdate.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
Pointer in which decrypted data will be stored.
Expected size of the data in bytes whereas the number of bytes provided should be multiples of 4.
Pointer to the GCM tag which needs to be verified during decryption of the data.

simplesect.para: None
If all of the data to be decrypted is available, the XSecure_AesDecryptData function can be used instead.

listitem.para: Clear AES contents by resetting it.
Clear AES_KEY_CLEAR bits to avoid clearing of key
Helion AES block expects the key in big-endian.
Enable CSU DMA Src channel for byte swapping.
Configure the CSU DMA Tx/Rx for the incoming Block
Push IV into the AES engine.

briefdescription.para: This function decrypts the encrypted data passed in and updates the GCM tag from any previous calls.

detaileddescription.para: The size from XSecure_AesDecryptInit is decremented from the size passed into this function to determine when the GCM tag passed to XSecure_AesDecryptInit needs to be compared to the GCM tag calculated in the AES engine.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
Pointer to the encrypted data which needs to be decrypted.
Expected size of data to be decrypted in bytes, whereas the number of bytes should be multiples of 4.

simplesect.para:
Final call of this API returns the status of GCM tag matching.
XSECURE_CSU_AES_GCM_TAG_MISMATCH: If GCM tag is mismatched
XSECURE_CSU_AES_ZEROIZATION_ERROR: If GCM tag is mismatched, zeroize the decrypted data and send the status of zeroization.
XST_SUCCESS: If GCM tag is matching.

When Size of the data equals to size of the remaining data that data will be treated as final data. This API can be called multpile times but sum of all Sizes should be equal to Size mention in init. Return of the final call of this API tells whether GCM tag is matching or not.

listitem.para: Check if this is final update
Wait for the Src DMA completion.
If this is the last update for the data
Wait for the Src DMA completion.
Disable CSU DMA Src channel for byte swapping.
Disable CSU DMA Dst channel for byte swapping.
Get the AES status to know if GCM check passed.
Aes engine is set under reset when GCM tag is failed or when the next block length of decryption is zero

briefdescription.para: This function decrypts the encrypted data provided and updates the DecData buffer with decrypted data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
Pointer to a buffer in which decrypted data will be stored.
Pointer to the encrypted data which needs to be decrypted.
Size of data to be decrypted in bytes, whereas the number of bytes should be multiples of 4.
Size of data to be decrypted in bytes, whereas the number of bytes should be multiples of 4.
Pointer to a buffer in which GCM tag address will be stored.

simplesect.para:
This API returns the status of GCM tag matching.
XSECURE_CSU_AES_GCM_TAG_MISMATCH: If GCM tag was mismatched
XST_SUCCESS: If GCM tag was matched.

When using this function to decrypt data that was encrypted with XSecure_AesEncryptData, the GCM tag will be stored as the last sixteen (16) bytes of data in XSecure_AesEncryptData's Dst (destination) buffer and should be used as the GcmTagAddr's pointer.

briefdescription.para: This API enables/disables data chunking.

detaileddescription.para: Chunking will be used when complete encrypted data is not present at a single contiguous location (for eg. DDR less systems.) or the data source is not directly reachable through CSU DMA.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
Used to enable or disable data chunking.

simplesect.para: None
Chunking enable will be taken account only for XSecure_AesDecrypt() API usage.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, u8 *ReadBuffer, u32 ChunkSize, u32(*DeviceCopy)(u32, UINTPTR, u32))
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesSetChunkConfig

briefdescription.para: This function sets the configuration for Data Chunking.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
Buffer where the data will be written after copying.
Length of the buffer in bytes.

Function pointer to copy data from FLASH to buffer. Arguments are:
SrcAddress: Address of data in device
DestAddress: Address where data will be copied
Length: Length of data in bytes. Return value should be 0 in case of success and 1 in case of failure.

simplesect.para: None

This function should be used along with XSecure_AesSetChunkConfig() API, this feature is taken into account only for XSecure_AesDecrypt() API while decrypting the boot image's partition which is generated using bootgen.

listitem.para: Chunk Size has to be multiple of words

briefdescription.para: This function sets and then clears the AES-GCM's reset line.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the AES key storage registers.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.

simplesect.para: None

briefdescription.para: This function configures and load AES key from selected key source.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.

simplesect.para: None

briefdescription.para: This is a helper function to decrypt chunked bitstream block and route to PCAP.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
Pointer to the encrypted bitstream block start.
Length of bitstream data block in bytes, whereas the number of bytes should be multiples of 4.

simplesect.para: returns XST_SUCCESS if bitstream block is decrypted by AES.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, u8 *Dst, const u8 *Src, const u8 *Tag, u32 Len, u32 Flag)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesDecryptBlk

briefdescription.para: This function handles decryption using the AES engine.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
Pointer to location where decrypted data will be written
Pointer to encrypted input data
Pointer to the GCM tag used for authentication
Length of the output data (in bytes)expected after decryption, whereas the number of bytes should be multiple of 4.
Denotes whether the block is Secure header or data block 0 : Secure Header 1 : Data Block / image

simplesect.para: returns XST_SUCCESS if GCM tag matching was successful

listitem.para: Push IV into the AES engine.
Enable CSU DMA Src channel for byte swapping.
Configure the CSU DMA Tx/Rx for the incoming Block.
Wait for the Dst DMA completion.
Disable CSU DMA Dst channel for byte swapping
Wait for the Src DMA completion.
Copy all the chunks to OCM, decrypt & send to PCAP
update address to point to incoming secure header
Copy the secure header and GCM tag from flash to OCM
Disable CSU DMA Src channel for byte swapping.
Get the AES status to know if GCM check passed.

briefdescription.para: This function will handle the AES-GCM Decryption.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
Pointer to encrypted data source location
Pointer to location where decrypted data will be written.
Expected total length of decrypted image expected.

simplesect.para: returns XST_SUCCESS if successful, or the relevant errorcode.
This function is used for decrypting the Image's partition encrypted by Bootgen

listitem.para: Chunking is only for bitstream partitions
Clear AES contents by resetting it.
Clear AES_KEY_CLEAR bits to avoid clearing of key
Helion AES block expects the key in big-endian.
Enable CSU DMA Src channel for byte swapping.
First block is always secure header
If decryption failed then return error.
After secure header, rest of the blocks are data blocks (image blocks)
Point IV to the CSU IV register.
Update the GcmTagAddr to get GCM-TAG for next block.

briefdescription.para: This file contains the implementation of the interface functions for AES driver.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the header file xsecure_aes.h for more detailed information.

para.preformatted: Ver Who Date Changes ----- --- -------- ------------------------------------------------------- 1.00 ba 09/10/14 Initial release 1.1 ba 11/10/15 Modified Key loading logic in AES encryption 1.1 ba 12/22/15 Added Chunking support in decryption 2.0 vns 01/28/17 Added APIs for decryption which can be used for generic decryption. vns 02/03/17 Added APIs for encryption in generic way. Removed existing XSecure_AesEncrypt API Modified encryption and decryption APIs such that all inputs will be accepted in little endian format(KEY, IV and Data). 2.2 vns 07/06/16 Added doxygen tags 3.0 vns 01/03/18 Modified XSecure_AesDecrypt() API to use key and IV placed in secure header by bootgen to decrypt the actual partition. vns 02/19/18 Modified XSecure_AesKeyZero() to clear KUP and AES key Added XSecure_AesKeyZero() call in XSecure_AesDecrypt() API to clear keys. 4.0 arc 12/18/18 Fixed MISRA-C violations. arc 03/06/19 Added asserts to validate input params vns 03/13/19 As part of refactoring modified SSS configurations arc 03/20/19 Added time outs and status info for API's. mmd 03/15/19 Refactored the code. psl 03/26/19 Fixed MISRA-C violation 4.1 kal 05/20/19 Updated doxygen tags psl 07/02/19 Fixed Coverity warning. mmd 07/05/19 Optimized the code psl 07/31/19 Fixed MISRA-C violation kal 08/27/19 Changed default status to XST_FAILURE 4.2 ana 04/02/20 Skipped zeroization when destination is PCAP Added support of release and set reset for AES kpt 04/10/20 Resolved coverity warnings kal 10/07/20 Added KUP key clearing after use or in case of failure 4.5 bsv 04/01/21 Added support to encrypt bitstream to memory in chunks and then write to PCAP bsv 05/03/21 Add provision to load bitstream from OCM with DDR present in design 4.6 kal 08/11/21 Added EXPORT CONTROL eFuse check in AesInitialize am 09/17/21 Resolved compiler warnings 4.7 am 11/26/21 Resolved doxygen warnings am 12/14/21 Fixed input validation of InstancePtr in XSecure_AesChunkDecrypt function 5.2 ng 07/05/23 Added support for system device tree flow 5.3 vss 10/18/23 Fixed INIT_TO_NULL coverity warning ss 04/05/24 Fixed doxygen warnings
